scenarios.opening_query: Our food co-op's biggest supplier just demanded we sign a new contract with terms that could sink us.
scenarios.hidden_fact: They've also cut deliveries mid-contract to force the signature — the existing agreement runs eighteen more months, and the breach-and-coercion case is live now.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They cut deliveries mid-contract to force the signature — the current deal runs eighteen more months, so the breach case is live.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether coercive breach of the existing contract is underway
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Deliveries continue fine — the old contract genuinely expires next month, and negotiating the renewal terms is the work.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Deliveries continue — the old deal really does expire next month. It's a negotiation.

scenarios.opening_query: I want to turn my food blog into a real business before the brand deals get bigger.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities) || Business and Corporate > Publishing
scenarios.hidden_fact: The immediate driver is a cookbook deal — a publisher's contract is on my desk with rights, royalties, and option clauses I don't understand; the publishing agreement leads.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A cookbook contract is on my desk — rights, royalties, option clauses. The publishing deal leads.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a publishing contract is the immediate matter
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No book deal — it's the LLC, trademark filing, and sponsor-contract templates: formation work.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No book yet — LLC, trademark, sponsor templates. Formation work.

scenarios.opening_query: Our winery's distributor stopped paying and keeps our inventory locked in their warehouse.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Litigation || Business and Corporate > Restaurants/OLCC
scenarios.hidden_fact: Liquor-franchise law changes everything — our state's alcohol distribution statutes govern termination and inventory recovery, and the OLCC process is the first venue.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Alcohol-franchise statutes govern termination and inventory recovery — the OLCC process is the first venue.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether alcohol-distribution law governs the fight
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We're out of state and unregulated on this deal — a straight commercial contract and conversion suit.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: This deal's outside the regulated system — straight commercial suit.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex-employee started a competing shop and half my crew followed him within a month.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Litigation || Labor & Employment > General - Employer
scenarios.hidden_fact: There are no covenants — nobody signed anything; my exposure now is MY remaining crew's retention: enforceable agreements, pay structures, and not stepping on protected-activity rules while I rebuild.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Nobody ever signed covenants — the work now is retention: enforceable agreements and pay structures for the crew I still have.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether covenants exist to enforce or employer-side rebuilding is the need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Everyone signed two-year non-solicits — and his texts recruiting my crew violate them from day one; the enforcement case is ready.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Everyone signed non-solicits — and his recruiting texts violate them. Enforcement is ready.
scenarios.vetting_note: Covenant-existence; employer-side label used honestly (user's role stated openly).

scenarios.opening_query: The city's new ordinance bans the food carts from our pod's corner after twelve years.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > General (Local/Municipal) || Real Property > Landlord Commercial
scenarios.hidden_fact: I OWN the pod lot — eight cart tenants pay me monthly, and the ordinance guts my commercial-landlord business; my standing and claims run through that property interest.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I own the pod lot with eight cart tenants — the ordinance guts my landlord business, and my claims run through that property interest.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user's property-owner standing frames the challenge
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I'm one of the cart owners — the operators' ordinance challenge and grandfathering fight is my lane.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I run one of the carts — the operators' ordinance challenge is my lane.

scenarios.opening_query: A national chain wants to buy our family hardware store and Dad's ready to sign whatever they slide over.
scenarios.initial: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business || Real Property > General (Commercial)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The twist: they only want the real estate — the corner lot — not the business; it's a commercial property sale with a leaseback option, and the property deal is the whole transaction.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They only want the corner lot, not the business — a commercial property sale with a leaseback. That's the whole deal.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the transaction is realty rather than the business
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They want the whole operation — name, inventory, goodwill, staff — a true business acquisition needing full deal counsel.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They want everything — name, inventory, goodwill, staff. A true acquisition.
